stem: A healthcare operations study measures the wait time (minutes) for a sample of 30 emergency room visitors. The sample mean is 57.9 min with a sample standard deviation of 13.8 min. Assuming the data are approximately normally distributed, estimate the true population mean and provide a 95% confidence interval.

ground_truth.answer: Population mean ≈ 57.9 min. 95% CI = [52.8, 63.0]. Point estimate: 57.9 min.

common_failure_modes: Using z-critical value instead of t-critical for small samples
Not reporting the confidence interval alongside the point estimate
